observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize all procurements of inventory items under the Inventory account, except those purchased through the petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and to properly maintain SLC pursuant to Manual on NGAS.

4. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review, and as required under Section 74 PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of delayed production of the bank statements, securing snapshots as of a given date will do.

5. We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the submission of POs/contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting documents and the AIRs within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended and the Municipal Government agreed to require
the Inventory Committee the conduct of physical count of Property,
Plant and Equipment to reconcile with the Accounting records and
immediately submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Government agreed to require
the:
a) GSO to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road
Map and keep an updated complete LRN Property Card for all
roads and its components using the prescribed template;
b) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects; and
c) Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to
record the inventory of the local road network and its
components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network
Ledger Card and to religiously update the subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network and prepare a
lapsing schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end
of each year. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in
the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended and the Municipal Government agreed to require
the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger detailing all
the information under Loans Payables – Domestic account and to
submit the loan agreement and other related documents to facilitate
review of claim on loan repayments and to establish correct balances
of the accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Government agreed to require
the following:
a) Copies of the MOA by and between MPW-BARMM and the
Municipal Government be immediately submitted to the
Auditors’ Office and ensure the submission of copies of all other
MOA/contracts of fund transfers from Other Government
Offices/NGOs within five (5) working days from perfection
thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
b) Strict compliance with the submission of RCI, ROD,
Disbursement Vouchers/payrolls and its supporting documents
to the Auditors’ Office within the prescribed deadline pursuant
to COA Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Municipal
Government agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate
the preparation and prompt submission of the monthly BRS to the
Audit Team for review as required under Section 74 PD No. 1445 and
Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of delayed production
of the bank statements, securing a snapshots as of a given date will do.

We recommended and the Municipal Government agreed to instruct
the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD accomplishment report
accompanied with its supporting documents to the Office of the
Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused
policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan
pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
1. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of the above PPE to reflect the correct carrying amounts.

3. We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant the submission of the RCD with official receipts and validated deposit slips to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

6. We recommended that the Accounting Department prepare the separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.
7. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements

8. Reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts that had been already credited in Cash In Bank account but checks issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of December 31, 2020.

2. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements; and
3. Reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts that had been already credited in Bank account but checks issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of December 31, 2020.

5. We recommended that the Accounting Department prepare the separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.
1.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of their PPEs in order to reflect its correct carrying amounts.

2.8 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize all procurements of inventory items under the Inventory account, except those purchased through the petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and to properly maintain SLC pursuant to Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant the submission of RCD with official receipts and validated deposit slips to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

4.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the monthly BRS and other reports/documents to the Audit Team for their review, and as required under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and other COA Circulars. In case of delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snapshots as of a given date will do.

5.5 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the submission of POs/contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting documents and the AIRs within 24 hours from acceptance of goods, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

1. We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee, composed of a representative from the Mayor’s Office, the General Services Office/or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and in other departments and conduct the required annual physical count of property, plant and equipment of the municipality.

4. We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare Goods for Distribution.

6. We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
1) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis pursuant to the above cited regulations.

2) We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with its supporting documents of gender responsive and/or GAD of the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

4) We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete Physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

7) We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements and reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts paid in cash where Cash -in-Bank had been already credited but checks issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment to Accounts Payable.

10)We recommended to initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

1.6 We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee,
composed of a representative from the Mayor’s Office, the General
Services Office/or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and in other
departments and conduct the required annual physical count of
property, plant and equipment of the municipality.

2.3 We recommended that Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation
Statements; and
b. Reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts that had
been already credited in Cash In Bank account but checks
issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for
payment as of December 31, 2020.

3.9 We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General
Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for
Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the
Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare
Goods for Distribution.

4.6 We recommended that the Accounting Department prepare the
separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund,
most importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that
will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

5.4 We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person
to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied
with its supporting documents to the Office of the Office of the
Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused
policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan
pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
3) We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month, with or without receipt or disbursement of funds, as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002
5) Settle the Audit suspension.

6) The reliability of the inventory account balance of ₱ 50,300.00 Office Supplies Inventory as of December 31, 2018 could not be ascertained as to existence due to non-conduct of complete physical count and non-maintenance of supplies ledger cards and supplies stock cards, further this account is not moving for 6 years.

8) We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current Portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the Financial Statement, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

9) We recommended that the concerned officials of the municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
11)Settle the Audit suspension.

1. The reliability of the inventory account balance of ₱ 50,300.00 Office Supplies Inventory as of December 31, 2018 could not be ascertained as to existence due to non- conduct of complete physical count and non-maintenance of supplies ledger cards and supplies stock cards, further this account is not moving for 6 years.

2. We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements and reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts paid in cash where Cash -in-Bank had been already credited but checks issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of December 31, 2018 to Accounts Payable.

3. We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current Portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the Financial Statement, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

4. We recommended that the concerned officials of the municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit
the required Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis
pursuant to the above cited regulations.

2.5 We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to
prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with
its supporting documents to of gender responsive and/or GAD the
Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on
GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved
GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

3.3 We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant
prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month, with or without receipt or
disbursement of funds, as required by COA Circular No. 2012.

4.6 We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete
Physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an
Inventory Committee composed of representative each from the
Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to
prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of
the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and
inventories owned by the Municipality.

5. We recommended to initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management strictly require the preparation and approval of the PO before any payment is made on the purchase of construction materials for projects implemented by the Municipal Government in compliance on the above COA Circular.

We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete Physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Supply Officer to record all purchases of office supplies to the Office Supplies Inventory account, and Food Supplies Inventory account as provided under the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories. The Supply Officer should be instructed to prepare the RSMI for all issuances of office supplies and submit the same to the Accounting Office, on a monthly basis for the preparation of the JEV to take up the expense account.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipality established and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended that the Municipality shall ensure that the Accountant prepares the Report of LDRRMF every month whether there is transaction/ utilization for that month as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management requires the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and
second semester, respectively.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements and
reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts paid in cash where
Cash -in-Bank had been already credited but checks issued thereon
were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of December 31,
2018 to Accounts Payable.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor also to suspend payment of salary of all officers tasked to prepare bank reconciliation until said statement is made.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect
the current Portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt
in the Financial Statement, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of
the status of its obligation.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the municipality establish
and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and
gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and
Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete
physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an
Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the
Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare
the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical
count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned
by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall record and post in the Cashbook and the books of accounts Respectively the fund transfer amounting to P816,000.00 and adjust the cash and cash equivalent accounts so as to present the financial statements of the agency fairly presented and free from material misstatements. Furthermore, strictly observe the procedures and process of collections and deposits of income and revenue.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be directed to cause the immediate submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits covering the P 525,188.00 funds received/ collected during the year 2017 together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par.2) Rule VIII and Section 1, of Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.
We recommended appropriate action to settle the audit suspensions.

Request Technical Audit
Specialist from COA-ARMM,
Regional Office to inspect and
evaluate the above procurement
funded from the proceeds of
Loan from Philippine Veterans
Bank  and adhere to the
provision of Section 18 of P.D.
1445.

We recommend that the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant be directed to cause
the immediate submission of the
Report of Collections and
Deposits covering the
P253,525.31 funds
received/collected during the
year 2016 together with the
supporting official receipts
and validated deposit slips.

Encourage and urge the Local
Chief Executive to establish a
sound ecological solid waste
management systems and
constitute the Municipality
Solid Waste Management (SWM)
Boards in pursuant to Section 2
(par. 2), Rule VIII and Section
1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommend that  the Municipal Treasurer and  the Municipal Accountant shall record and post in the Cashbook and the books of accounts respectively the fund transfer amounting to P816,000.00 and adjust the cash and cash equivalent accounts so as to present the financial statements of the agency fairly presented and free from material misstatements. Furthermore, strictly observe the procedures and process of collections and deposits of income and revenue.

We recommend that Management strictly require the preparation and approval of the PO before any payment is made on the purchase of construction materials for projects implemented by the Municipal Government in compliance on the above COA Circular.

We  recommend  that the  Municipal  Treasurer   and   the   Municipal   Accountant    be directed  to  cause  the  immediate  submission  of  the  Report  of   Collections    and    Deposits covering  the  P525,188.00   funds   received/collected   during   the  year   2017   together  with  the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

We recommend that Management initiate the conduct of a complete Physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

We recommend that Management require the Supply Officer to record all purchases of office supplies to the Office Supplies Inventory account, and Food supplies Inventory account as provided under the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories.
The Supply Officer should be instructed to prepare the RSMI for all issuances of office supplies and submit the same to the Accounting Office, on a monthly basis for the preparation of the JEV to take up the expense account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive established a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2) Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommend that the concerned officials of the municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommend that the Municipality shall ensure that the Accountant prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month whether there is transaction/ utilization for that month as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the previous
administration of the
municipality shall cause the
official documentation of the
turnover of offices between and
among the outgoing and the
incoming officials of the
municipality with the
preparation of invoices and
receipt of property and
responsibility accountabilities
to determine properly their
respective responsibilities

We recommended that officials
concerned were advise to comply
within 90 days with the
requirements of the
transactions suspended in audit
and submit lacking supporting
papers. Otherwise, it will be
disallowed in audit and
Settlement thereof should be
immediately enforced by
withholding the salaries or
other claims of the persons
liable in satisfaction of the
amounts disallowed which is
final and executory.

We recommend that management
should ensure that due
notification   to the Auditor
of the time and date of every
scheduled delivery of goods and
services be made on time
pursuant to COA Circular No.
95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Issue demand letters to the implementing agencies (IAs) and require them to submit Reports of Expenditures relative to the disbursement of funds duly verified by the Auditor assigned thereat;

Enforce compliance with rules and regulations of granting and liquidation of fund transferred to implementing agencies under COA Circular No. 94-013.
We recommend that all assets of the agency shall be taken up in the books of the accounts and adhere to the provision of section 111 of presidential Decree No. 1145 and the PPSAS Chart of Accounts.

Recommendation:
Request Technical Audit Specialist from COA-ARMM, Regional Office to inspect and evaluate the above procurement funded from the proceeds of Loan from Philippine Veterans Bank  and adhere to the provision of Section 18 of P.D.  1445.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the previous administration of the municipality shall cause the official documentation of the turnover of offices between and among the outgoing and the incoming officials of the municipality with the preparation of invoices and receipt of property and responsibility accountabilities to determine properly their respective responsibilities

Recommendation:
We recommended that officials concerned were advise to comply within 90 days with the requirements of the transactions suspended  in  audit  and  submit
lacking supporting papers. Otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit and Settlement thereof should be immediately enforced by withholding the salaries or other claims of the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should ensure that due notification   to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and  services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be directed to cause the immediate submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits covering the P253,525.31 funds received/collected during the year 2016 together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

Recommendation:
Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommend that  the  Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be directed to cause the immediate submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits covering the P123,959.60 funds received/collected during the year 2015 together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

We recommend that management in the succeeding fiscal years, shall include in the Annual Appropriations for Senior citizens and persons wit disabilities and 2% for the implementation of the programs of the Local Council for the protection of children as required under RA 9442 amending RA 7277 so that PAPS for them can be perfectly executed.

Request  Technical  Audit         Specialist   from    COA-ARMM,    Regional   Office   to   inspect  and evaluate the  above           accomplishment and adhere to the  provision of Section 18 of P.D.   1445.
